title: Implement temporal.testing.history namespace
content:
# Implement temporal.testing.history namespace

Implements [[s-5c1w]].

## Overview

Create `src/temporal/testing/history.clj` — the foundational history-loading and capture namespace. This has no dependencies on other new issues.

## Functions to implement

| Function | Wraps | Notes |
|----------|-------|-------|
| `from-json` | `WorkflowExecutionHistory/fromJson(String)` | 2-arity `[json workflow-id]` overload for explicit id override |
| `from-file` | slurp → `fromJson` | Accept `java.io.File`, `java.nio.Path`, or string path |
| `from-resource` | `(io/resource name)` → slurp → `fromJson` | Throw clear `ex-info` if resource not found |
| `fetch` | `(.fetchHistory ^WorkflowClient client workflow-id)` | `client` is a temporal.client.core WorkflowClient |
| `->json` | `(.toJson history pretty-print?)` | Default `pretty-print?` = `true` |

## Datafiable extension

```clojure
(extend-protocol p/Datafiable
  WorkflowExecutionHistory
  (datafy [h]
    {:workflow-id (.. h getWorkflowExecution getWorkflowId)
     :run-id      (.. h getWorkflowExecution getRunId)
     :events      (.getEvents h)}))
```

## Java imports needed

```clojure
(import '[io.temporal.internal.common WorkflowExecutionHistory])
```

No new Maven dependency — `WorkflowExecutionHistory` is bundled in `temporal-shaded 1.32.1`.

## Conventions

- Namespace docstring: `"Methods for loading, capturing, and serializing Temporal workflow execution histories for use in replay testing."`
- All public fns: markdown docstring with one-line summary, `Arguments:` table, fenced `clojure` example, `[[cross-refs]]` to related fns
- Internal helpers: `^:no-doc`
- Model on `src/temporal/testing/env.clj`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All 5 functions implemented and documented
- [ ] `Datafiable` extension: `(d/datafy (history/from-json json-str))` returns map
- [ ] `from-resource` throws clear `ex-info` when resource not found
- [ ] `from-file` accepts string, `File`, and `Path`
- [ ] No new Maven dependencies

title: Implement temporal.testing.replayer core (create/replay/close/replay-history)
content:
# Implement temporal.testing.replayer core

Implements [[s-5c1w]]. Blocked by [[i-850r]].

## Overview

Create `src/temporal/testing/replayer.clj`. Provides a reusable replayer handle (TestWorkflowEnvironment + worker via dynamic-dispatch path) and single-history replay with Clojure-friendly error handling.

## `replayer-options` spec-map (internal)

Follow the SDK idiom from `src/temporal/internal/utils.clj`:

```clojure
(def ^:no-doc replayer-option-spec {})  ;; extend if WorkflowReplayerOptions has useful builder methods

(defn ^:no-doc replayer-options-> [params]
  (u/build (WorkflowReplayerOptions/newBuilder) replayer-option-spec params))
```

Expose `replayer-options` as a public var (the spec map) for documentation, matching `temporal.client.worker/worker-options`.

## `create` — reusable replayer handle

Options map:
| Key | Description | Type | Default |
|-----|-------------|------|---------|
| `:dispatch` | Explicit workflow dispatch table | vector of symbols | auto-dispatch |
| `:data-converter` | Custom data converter | DataConverter | default |
| `:replayer-options` | WorkflowReplayerOptions params | map | `{}` |

- Build a `TestWorkflowEnvironment` (thread `:data-converter` through `workflow-client-options` as `temporal.testing.env/create` does)
- Get worker: `(.newWorker env task-queue worker-opts)`
- Call `temporal.client.worker/init` on the worker with dispatch table
- Start: `(.start env)`
- Return a `Closeable` record holding `{:env env :worker worker}`
- Use a generated internal task queue name (e.g. `(str "replay-" (java.util.UUID/randomUUID))`)

## `replay` — single history

```clojure
(defn replay [replayer history]
  (try
    (WorkflowReplayer/replayWorkflowExecution history (:worker replayer))
    (catch Exception e
      (throw (ex-info "Workflow replay failed: non-determinism detected"
                      {:workflow-id (.. history getWorkflowExecution getWorkflowId)
                       :cause e})))))
```

## `close` / `synchronized-stop`

- `close` → async: `(.shutdown (:env replayer))`
- `synchronized-stop` → blocking: `(.close (:env replayer))`
- `Closeable.close()` delegates to `synchronized-stop`

## `replay-history` — one-shot convenience

```clojure
(defn replay-history
  ([history] (replay-history history {}))
  ([history opts]
   (with-open [r (create opts)]
     (replay r history))))
```

## Java imports

```clojure
(import '[io.temporal.testing WorkflowReplayer TestWorkflowEnvironment])
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `create` builds a working env with dynamic dispatch registered
- [ ] `replay` passes on valid history, throws `ex-info` on non-determinism
- [ ] `with-open` works (Closeable implemented)
- [ ] `replay-history` one-shot works
- [ ] `:data-converter` and `:dispatch` options work correctly
- [ ] No new Maven dependencies

title: Implement replay-all and datafied ReplayResults
content:
# Implement replay-all and datafied ReplayResults

Implements [[s-5c1w]]. Blocked by [[i-b5o1]].

## Overview

Add `replay-all` to `temporal.testing.replayer` for batch replay (the CI use case), and add `Datafiable` protocol extensions for `ReplayResults` and `ReplayResults$ReplayError`.

## `replay-all` function

```clojure
(defn replay-all
  ([replayer histories] (replay-all replayer histories {}))
  ([replayer histories {:keys [fail-fast?] :or {fail-fast? false}}]
   (-> (WorkflowReplayer/replayWorkflowExecutions histories fail-fast? (:worker replayer))
       d/datafy)))
```

Options:
| Key | Description | Type | Default |
|-----|-------------|------|---------|
| `:fail-fast?` | Stop on first failure | boolean | `false` |

Returns datafied map: `{:total n :failures [{:workflow-id "..." :error #<Exception>}]}`

## Datafiable extensions

```clojure
(extend-protocol p/Datafiable
  ReplayResults
  (datafy [r]
    {:total    (+ (count (.getReplayErrors r)) (count (.getSuccessfulExecutions r)))
     :failures (mapv d/datafy (.getReplayErrors r))})

  ReplayResults$ReplayError
  (datafy [e]
    {:workflow-id (.getWorkflowId e)
     :error       (.getException e)}))
```

Verify actual method names on `ReplayResults` via `javap` before implementing.

## Java imports

```clojure
(import '[io.temporal.testing WorkflowReplayer ReplayResults])
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Successful batch returns `{:total n :failures []}`
- [ ] Failed batch includes offending workflow-id in `:failures`
- [ ] `fail-fast? true` stops on first failure
- [ ] `fail-fast? false` collects all failures
- [ ] `(datafy results)` works at the REPL
- [ ] Return value is a plain Clojure map

title: Add tests for replay testing API
content:
# Add tests for replay testing API

Implements [[s-5c1w]]. Blocked by [[i-b5o1]] and [[i-9rsa]].

## New test files

### `test/temporal/test/replay_history_test.clj`
Unit tests for `temporal.testing.history` (no live Temporal environment needed):

- `from-json` → `->json` round-trip: parse JSON string, serialize back, re-parse, assert workflow-id preserved
- `from-resource` with real JSON fixture in `test/resources/histories/`
- `from-resource` with missing resource throws `ex-info` with clear message
- `from-file` with a temp file
- `(datafy history)` returns map with `:workflow-id` and `:events`

Include fixture at `test/resources/histories/sample.json`.

### `test/temporal/test/replay_test.clj`
Integration tests using the `temporal.test.utils` fixture:

1. **Successful single replay:** Run a deterministic workflow, capture history with `history/fetch`, replay with `replayer/replay` — must not throw.
2. **Non-determinism detected:** Replay the same history against a modified workflow — must throw `ex-info` with `:workflow-id` in ex-data.
3. **`replay-all` all pass:** Replay 2-3 histories; assert `{:failures []}`.
4. **`replay-all` partial failure, collect-all:** `fail-fast? false`; assert `:failures` has one entry with correct `:workflow-id`.
5. **`replay-all` fail-fast:** `fail-fast? true`; assert stops early.
6. **`with-open` lifecycle:** Verify env is shut down after `with-open` block.

### Refactor `test/temporal/test/versioning.clj`
Replace raw Java interop at lines 49-56:
```clojure
;; Before:
(let [history (.fetchHistory client "test-1")]
  (.replayWorkflowExecution worker history))
;; After:
(let [h (history/fetch client "test-1")]
  (replayer/replay-history h))
```

## Conventions

```clojure
(ns temporal.test.replay-test
  (:require [clojure.test :refer [deftest testing is use-fixtures]]
            [temporal.test.utils :as t]
            [temporal.testing.history :as history]
            [temporal.testing.replayer :as replayer]))

(use-fixtures :once t/wrap-service)
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All unit tests pass without a live Temporal env
- [ ] All integration tests pass with the in-memory test env
- [ ] Refactored `versioning.clj` still passes all original assertions
- [ ] `lein test` (full suite) stays green
- [ ] Sample fixture `test/resources/histories/sample.json` checked in

title: Write replay testing documentation guide
content:
# Write replay testing documentation guide

Implements [[s-5c1w]]. Blocked by [[i-850r]], [[i-b5o1]], and [[i-9rsa]].

## Deliverables

### 1. Namespace and function docstrings

Both `temporal.testing.history` and `temporal.testing.replayer` must have:
- Namespace-level docstring explaining purpose and relationship between the two namespaces
- Per-function markdown docstrings: one-line summary, `Arguments:` table (`| Key | Description | Type | Default |`), fenced `clojure` example, `[[cross-refs]]`
- Model on `src/temporal/testing/env.clj`

### 2. `doc/replay_testing.md` guide

Structure:
```
# Replay Testing
## Why replay testing?
## The capture-then-replay workflow
## Loading and capturing histories
## Replaying a history
## Batch replay in CI
## Connection to versioning
## API reference
```

Link conventions:
- Upstream concepts: `[Temporal Testing Suite](https://docs.temporal.io/develop/java/testing-suite)`
- SDK API refs: `[temporal.testing.history/from-json](https://cljdoc.org/d/io.github.manetu/temporal-sdk/CURRENT/api/temporal.testing.history#from-json)`

### 3. `doc/cljdoc.edn` TOC entry

Add immediately after `["Testing" {:file "doc/testing.md"}]`:

```clojure
["Replay Testing" {:file "doc/replay_testing.md"}]
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `doc/replay_testing.md` exists and covers all sections
- [ ] All upstream links use `docs.temporal.io` URLs
- [ ] All SDK API links use `cljdoc.org/.../CURRENT/api/...` pattern
- [ ] `doc/cljdoc.edn` updated with TOC entry
- [ ] All public fns in both namespaces have proper docstrings
- [ ] Guide links back to `doc/workflows.md` versioning section
- [ ] `lein codox` renders without errors

title: Add replay-testing runnable sample
content:
# Add replay-testing runnable sample

Implements [[s-5c1w]]. Blocked by [[i-850r]], [[i-b5o1]], and [[i-9rsa]].

## Project structure

```
samples/replay-testing/
├── project.clj
├── README.md
├── src/temporal/sample/replay_testing/core.clj
└── test/
    ├── resources/histories/order.json   (pre-captured fixture)
    └── temporal/sample/replay_testing/core_test.clj
```

## `src/.../core.clj`

A simple but realistic workflow demonstrating what replay testing protects:
- `defworkflow` with one or two `defactivity` calls (e.g. `validate-order`, `charge-payment`)
- Realistic enough to show the value; simple enough to follow

## `test/.../core_test.clj`

Full capture-then-replay cycle:

```clojure
(deftest replay-captures-determinism
  (testing "A valid history replays cleanly"
    (let [result @(c/get-result (c/start wf {:order-id "ord-1"}))]
      (is (= :ok (:status result)))
      (let [h (history/fetch client (workflow-id wf))]
        (spit "test/resources/histories/order.json" (history/->json h true))
        (is (nil? (replayer/replay-history (history/from-file "test/resources/histories/order.json")))))))
  (testing "Replay detects non-determinism"
    ;; replay a history against a modified workflow; assert throws
    ...))
```

Pre-capture `test/resources/histories/order.json` so tests are runnable without a live server.

## `README.md`

Model on `samples/hello-world/README.md`. Include:
- What this sample demonstrates
- `lein run` (run the workflow)
- `lein test` (run the replay test)
- Brief explanation of capture-then-replay with link to cljdoc guide and https://docs.temporal.io/develop/java/testing-suite

## `project.clj`

Model on `samples/hello-world/project.clj`. Depend on current SDK version. Include `eftest` in `:dev` profile.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `samples/replay-testing/` exists with full project structure
- [ ] `lein test` passes in the sample directory
- [ ] Tests run against in-memory `TestWorkflowEnvironment` (no real server)
- [ ] Pre-captured `order.json` fixture checked in
- [ ] `README.md` explains the sample and links to docs
- [ ] Compiles against current SDK version

title: Bump temporal-shaded + temporal-opentracing to 1.36.0 and establish green baseline
content:
## Work

1. In `project.clj`, update:
   - Line 16: `[io.temporal/temporal-shaded "1.32.1"]` → `"1.36.0"`
   - Line 36: `[io.temporal/temporal-opentracing "1.32.1"]` → `"1.36.0"` (dev profile)
2. Regenerate `pom.xml` via `lein pom` (do not hand-edit — it is a generated artifact)
3. Confirm Java 11 compatibility: verify `project.clj:27` (`-source/-target 11`) is still supported by 1.36.0
4. Run `lein cljfmt check`, `lein cloverage`, `lein jar`
5. Triage any compile errors or test failures into a checklist; note which downstream issues each failure belongs to

## Notes
- This is the **foundation issue** — all other issues are blocked on this completing successfully
- The two version strings appear only in `project.clj:16` and `project.clj:36`; `pom.xml` is regenerated
- CI baseline: JDK 23, `lein cljfmt check` → `lein cloverage` → `lein jar`

title: Handle TLS auto-enable with API key (1.33 breaking change)
content:
## Background

Temporal Java SDK 1.33 changed behavior: **TLS is now automatically enabled when an API key is provided** via `.addApiKey()`. Previously, TLS was only enabled when `:enable-https true` was explicitly set.

The Clojure wrapper in `src/temporal/client/options.clj` (~111-128) maps `:api-key-fn` → `.addApiKey` and `:enable-https` as independent booleans (`:enable-https` defaults to false). After upgrade, setting `:api-key-fn` without `:enable-https true` will now silently enable TLS — a behavior change for any user connecting with an API key to a plaintext endpoint.

Additionally, `src/temporal/tls.clj` uses shaded netty import paths (`io.temporal.shaded.io.grpc.netty.shaded...`) which may change across SDK versions.

## Work

1. Audit `src/temporal/client/options.clj` (~111-128) and `src/temporal/tls.clj`: verify shaded import paths still valid in 1.36.0
2. Determine the correct wrapper behavior: document (or expose an option to disable) the auto-TLS-with-API-key behavior
3. Update `doc/clients.md` for the new TLS + API key interaction
4. Add/update test coverage in `test/temporal/test/tls.clj` for API-key + TLS scenarios
5. Check `test/temporal/test/types.clj:31,33,73,75` (exercises `:ssl-context` + `:enable-https false`)

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] No behavior regression for existing plaintext/TLS clients
- [ ] API key + TLS interaction documented and tested
- [ ] Shaded import paths in `tls.clj` verified valid for 1.36.0

title: Handle CAN search-attr carryover + expose CAN versioning options (1.33/1.34/1.36)
content:
## Background

Two related changes to Continue-As-New (CAN) across the upgrade range:

**Breaking (1.33):** Previous execution's search attributes are now **carried over** when none are explicitly set on CAN. Code relying on search attributes being cleared on CAN must now explicitly clear them. The wrapper maps `:search-attributes` → `.setSearchAttributes` in `src/temporal/workflow.clj:263-273`; the default (omit `:search-attributes`) now has different semantics.

**Additive (1.34, 1.36):** New CAN versioning options not yet exposed in the wrapper:
- `AUTO_UPGRADE` (1.34): PINNED workflows can upgrade to latest deployment on CAN
- `USE_RAMPING_VERSION` (1.36): Pins CAN workflow to task queue's Ramping Version at start

## Work

1. Investigate and document the 1.33 search-attribute carryover behavior change
2. Update `src/temporal/workflow.clj` `continue-as-new-option-spec` (lines 263-273) as needed
3. Expose `AUTO_UPGRADE` and `USE_RAMPING_VERSION` versioning options (new keyword → Java enum mapping)
4. Update/extend `test/temporal/test/continue_as_new.clj` (currently only tests basic restart, no search-attributes or versioning)
5. Update `doc/workflows.md` for CAN behavior changes and new versioning options

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Search-attribute carryover behavior change documented; tests updated/added
- [ ] `AUTO_UPGRADE` and `USE_RAMPING_VERSION` exposed and tested
- [ ] `continue_as_new.clj` tests cover search-attribute and versioning scenarios

title: Handle ActivityInfo nullable getters (1.35 breaking change)
content:
## Background

Temporal Java SDK 1.35 made several `ActivityInfo` getters `@Nullable` to support Standalone Activities (which have no associated workflow):
- `getWorkflowId()` — read at `src/temporal/internal/activity.clj:62`
- `getRunId()` — read at `src/temporal/internal/activity.clj:63`
- Also nullable but not currently read: `getWorkflowRunId()`, `getWorkflowType()`, `getWorkflowNamespace()`

The current `Datafiable` implementation in `internal/activity.clj` places these directly into a map. No NPE will occur, but `:workflow-id` and `:run-id` can now be `nil` for standalone activities. Downstream consumers may not expect nil values here.

## Work

1. Review `src/temporal/internal/activity.clj:57-69` (the `Datafiable ActivityInfo` extension)
2. Add `isInWorkflow()` predicate to the datafied map (or expose as a separate public function in `temporal.activity`) so consumers can detect the standalone vs. in-workflow case
3. Document the nil semantics clearly in `temporal.activity` docstrings
4. Update `test/temporal/test/activity_info.clj:14` to cover the within-workflow case explicitly
5. Consider adding a test for the standalone/nil case once the Standalone Activities wrapper ([[s-3lau]]) is implemented

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `isInWorkflow()` predicate exposed in the public API
- [ ] Docstrings document nil possibility for `:workflow-id`, `:run-id` in standalone context
- [ ] `activity_info.clj` test updated

title: Expose worker shutdown/heartbeat options (1.36)
content:
## Background

Temporal Java SDK 1.36 added two new options not yet exposed in the Clojure wrapper:

1. **`WorkerOptions.Builder.setAllowActivityHeartbeatDuringShutdown`** — removes the heartbeat limitation during graceful shutdown. Note: enabling this makes `ActivityWorkerShutdownException` detection impossible (trade-off to document).
2. **`WorkerFactoryOptions.Builder.setShutdownCheckInterval`** — controls the shutdown check polling interval, primarily useful for speeding up worker shutdown in testing (Temporal discourages use in production).

Current state: neither option is present in `src/temporal/client/worker.clj` (confirmed: the `worker-options` map ends around line 165 without these; `worker-factory-options` around line 56 also absent).

## Work

1. Add `:allow-activity-heartbeat-during-shutdown` → `.setAllowActivityHeartbeatDuringShutdown` to the `worker-options` spec in `src/temporal/client/worker.clj`
2. Add `:shutdown-check-interval` → `.setShutdownCheckInterval` to `worker-factory-options`
3. Add appropriate docstrings noting the trade-offs (heartbeat option makes `ActivityWorkerShutdownException` undetectable; shutdown-check-interval is test-only)
4. Update `doc/workers.md`
5. Add tests for the new option paths (at minimum, verify the options are accepted without error)

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Both options exposed with docstrings noting trade-offs
- [ ] Tests pass
- [ ] `doc/workers.md` updated

title: Expose ActivityExecutionContext.getCancellationToken() (1.36)
content:
## Background

Temporal Java SDK 1.36 added `ActivityExecutionContext.getCancellationToken()` — a new way to detect activity cancellation **without** waiting for a heartbeat response. Previously, the only way to detect cancellation was to call heartbeat and catch `ActivityCanceledException`. This new API allows detection even in activities that don't heartbeat, and requires a recent Temporal server version.

The Clojure wrapper exposes activity context via `temporal.activity` (public API) backed by `temporal.internal.activity`.

## Work

1. Determine where to expose `getCancellationToken()` — likely in `temporal.activity` alongside the existing heartbeat functions
2. Decide on idiomatic Clojure surface: return the raw `CancellationToken` (a `java.util.concurrent.Future<Void>`), or wrap it (e.g. as a promise/channel that delivers on cancellation)
3. Implement and add docstring noting the server version requirement
4. Add test coverage in the activity tests (e.g. extend `test/temporal/test/heartbeat.clj` or add a new `cancellation_token.clj` test)
5. Update `doc/activities.md`

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `getCancellationToken()` accessible from `temporal.activity` namespace
- [ ] Idiomatic Clojure surface documented
- [ ] Tests pass
- [ ] Server version prerequisite noted in docstring and docs

feedback.content:
Implemented `get-cancellation-token` in `temporal.activity`:

- **API surface**: Returns the raw `ActivityCancellationToken` Java object (not a promise/channel) so users can choose `.isCancellationRequested` (polling), `.throwIfCancellationRequested` (throw-on-cancel), or `.getCancellationFuture` (CompletableFuture for async). This matches the existing pattern where `get-heartbeat-details` returns a raw value and lets callers decide what to do with it.
- **Implementation**: One-liner in `src/temporal/activity.clj` — calls `(Activity/getExecutionContext)` (same approach as `heartbeat` and `get-heartbeat-details`) then `.getCancellationToken`. No changes needed in `temporal.internal.activity`.
- **Tests**: `test/temporal/test/cancellation_token.clj` — activity calls `get-cancellation-token`, asserts token is not cancelled and future not done in the normal (non-cancelled) path. 2 assertions, 0 failures.
- **Docs**: `doc/activities.md` — new "Cancellation" section with server version prerequisite note and examples for all three usage patterns (polling, throw, CompletableFuture).
- Server version prerequisite noted in both the docstring and documentation.

title: Expose Plugin system (Client/Worker/Schedule/Stubs plugins) (1.33)
content:
## Background

Temporal Java SDK 1.33 introduced a stable Plugin API that allows customizing SDK behavior via four plugin interfaces:
- `WorkflowServiceStubsPlugin` — customize gRPC stubs
- `WorkflowClientPlugin` — customize workflow client behavior
- `ScheduleClientPlugin` — customize schedule client behavior
- `WorkerPlugin` — customize worker behavior

These are currently unused in the Clojure wrapper. This issue covers designing and implementing idiomatic Clojure access to the plugin system.

## Work

1. **Design** (may deserve a short design note/PR description): how should plugins be expressed in Clojure? Options include:
   - Accepting pre-built Java plugin instances in the options maps (low friction, mirrors interceptors pattern)
   - Providing Clojure protocol/defrecord helpers to implement plugin interfaces
2. Add `:plugins` (or per-type keys) to the relevant options builders in `src/temporal/client/options.clj` and `src/temporal/client/worker.clj`
3. Document how to implement a plugin in Clojure (using `reify` or proxy)
4. Add a basic test (a no-op plugin that verifies it is called)
5. Update `doc/clients.md` and `doc/workers.md`

## Notes
- Interceptors (`WorkflowClientInterceptor`, `WorkerInterceptor`) are the existing extension point; plugins are a complementary, coarser-grained mechanism
- The simplest approach (accept Java plugin instances in options maps) mirrors how `:interceptors` works today

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Plugins accepted in client/worker/schedule options
- [ ] At least one test verifying plugin lifecycle
- [ ] Documented with a `reify` example

feedback.content:
## Plugin API implementation (i-6ord)

✅ **Requirements met:** All four plugin interfaces exposed via `:plugins` key in the relevant options maps.

**Design decision:** Chose the low-friction approach — accept pre-built Java plugin instances in the options maps, exactly mirroring how `:interceptors` works today. No protocol/defrecord wrappers were added; `reify` is idiomatic and sufficient.

**Changes:**
- `src/temporal/client/options.clj`: Added imports for `WorkflowClientPlugin`, `ScheduleClientPlugin`, `WorkflowServiceStubsPlugin`; added `:plugins` handler to `workflow-client-options`, `schedule-client-options`, and `stub-options` maps via `.setPlugins(into-array PluginType %2)`.
- `src/temporal/client/worker.clj`: Added import for `WorkerPlugin`; added `:plugins` to `worker-factory-options`.
- `test/temporal/client/options_test.clj`: Three new unit tests (`workflow-client-plugins`, `schedule-client-plugins`, `stub-plugins`) each creating a `reify`-based no-op plugin and verifying it is registered on the built options object.
- `doc/clients.md` / `doc/workers.md`: Added "Plugins" sections with `reify` examples covering all four plugin interfaces.

✅ **Evidence:** `lein test temporal.client.options-test` — 5 tests, 12 assertions, 0 failures/errors. `lein check` clean (existing pre-existing reflection warnings only).

title: Verify GZIP compression default + run gRPC CVE pass (1.33/1.35/1.36)
content:
## Background

Two security-relevant gRPC upgrades are bundled in this Java SDK range:
- **gRPC 1.75.0** (1.33) — resolves CVE-2025-55163 and CVE-2025-24970
- **gRPC 1.76.0** (1.35) — resolves network stream corruption with multiple Netty versions

Additionally, **1.36.0 enables GZIP gRPC transport compression by default**. This is transparent to most users but:
- May affect debugging (compressed traffic harder to inspect)
- Should be documented with the `GrpcCompression.NONE` opt-out via `ServiceStubsOptions`
- Should be verified against the in-process `TestWorkflowEnvironment` to ensure no interop issues

## Work

1. Run `/cve-remediation` after the dependency bump to confirm CVE-2025-55163 and CVE-2025-24970 are no longer present
2. Run `lein cloverage` (full test suite) and verify no GZIP-related failures in the test env
3. Add a note to `doc/clients.md` documenting the GZIP default and `GrpcCompression.NONE` opt-out
4. Optionally: verify the opt-out path actually works (a test or manual REPL check)

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] CVE scan confirms gRPC CVEs resolved
- [ ] Full test suite passes with GZIP default enabled
- [ ] `doc/clients.md` documents GZIP default and opt-out

feedback.content:
## i-4h86: GZIP CVE pass — complete

✅ **CVE scan (trivy)**: CVE-2025-55163 and CVE-2025-24970 are absent from `temporal-shaded` 1.36.0. Other findings (jackson-databind, aircompressor, json-smart) are transitive dependencies inside the shaded jar — not directly controllable.

✅ **Full test suite**: 108 tests, 357 assertions, 0 failures, 0 errors, 90.34% line coverage. No GZIP-related failures. The in-process `TestWorkflowEnvironment` is unaffected by the GZIP default.

✅ **SDK + docs**: Exposed `:grpc-compression` option in `temporal.client.options/stub-options` (backed by `ServiceStubsOptions$Builder.setGrpcCompression`). Documented the GZIP-by-default behavior and `GrpcCompression/NONE` opt-out in `doc/clients.md`.

📝 **Design decision**: The opt-out uses raw Java interop (`GrpcCompression/NONE`) — consistent with how other Java enum options are handled in this SDK (e.g. overlap policy). No wrapping layer added.

title: OpenTracing 1.36 integration review and alignment
content:
## Background

`temporal-opentracing` is declared as a dev dependency at `project.clj:36`. It is used only in `test/temporal/test/tracing.clj` via:
- `io.temporal.opentracing.OpenTracingClientInterceptor` (line 9)
- `io.temporal.opentracing.OpenTracingWorkerInterceptor` (line 9)

Temporal 1.36 shipped OpenTracing improvements including a new `OpenTracingActivityClientInterceptor` for standalone activities and OTel tracing for `startWithUpdate`.

The current tracing test (line 73) notes "todo: verify that tracing is working" — tracing is wired up but not functionally verified.

## Work

1. Bump `temporal-opentracing` to 1.36.0 (done by [[i-4833]]; verify it compiles and the tracing test passes)
2. Run `test/temporal/test/tracing.clj` and resolve any failures
3. Assess whether the "todo: verify that tracing is working" can now be properly addressed
4. Optionally: add `OpenTracingActivityClientInterceptor` wiring for standalone activity tracing (coordinate with [[s-3lau]])
5. Update `doc/workers.md` or `doc/clients.md` if OpenTracing setup has changed

## Acceptance criteria
- [x] `tracing.clj` tests pass with 1.36.0
- [x] The "todo: verify tracing" note resolved or escalated as a separate issue

## Resolution

- Tests pass with 1.36.0 — no compile or runtime failures.
- Resolved the "todo: verify tracing" by adding a `MockTracer` via `OpenTracingOptions` (`OpenTracingOptions/newBuilder` + `.setTracer`) and asserting that `StartWorkflow:traced-workflow` and `RunWorkflow:traced-workflow` spans are captured after workflow execution. Added `[io.opentracing/opentracing-mock "0.33.0"]` to dev dependencies.
- `OpenTracingActivityClientInterceptor` (new in 1.36.0) is for standalone activities and deferred to [[s-3lau]] / activity client implementation work.
- Added OpenTracing section to `doc/workers.md` documenting the `OpenTracingOptions` builder pattern and noting the future `OpenTracingActivityClientInterceptor` integration.

title: Track Standalone Nexus Operations (NexusClient) for future evaluation
content:
## Background

Temporal Java SDK 1.36.0 introduced **Standalone Nexus Operations (Experimental)** — a new `NexusClient` interface for interacting with Nexus services outside of workflows, and a `NexusServiceClient` generic interface for type-safe operation execution.

This is currently **Experimental** (Temporal's lowest stability tier), meaning the API may change significantly before stabilization. No Clojure wrapper work is appropriate until it reaches at least Public Preview.

## Purpose of this issue

Track this feature so it is not forgotten. Revisit when Temporal promotes Standalone Nexus to Public Preview or General Availability.

## Revisit criteria

- Temporal SDK releases Standalone Nexus as Public Preview or GA
- There is demonstrated demand from temporal-clojure-sdk users
- The Java API has stabilized enough to invest in a wrapper design

## Non-action

No implementation work. This issue should remain open until the revisit criteria are met, at which point create a new spec for the Nexus wrapper.

title: Update documentation and CHANGELOG for 1.36.0 upgrade
content:
## Work

Update all user-facing documentation to reflect changes introduced in the 1.33–1.36 upgrade range.

### `doc/workers.md`
- Add new worker shutdown/heartbeat options (`:allow-activity-heartbeat-during-shutdown`, `:shutdown-check-interval`)
- Drop "experimental" caveat from worker versioning (stable since 1.33)
- Drop "experimental" caveat from environment config (stable since 1.33)
- Add Plugin system usage example

### `doc/clients.md`
- Document TLS auto-enable with API key behavior (1.33) and the implications
- Document GZIP compression default (1.36) and `GrpcCompression.NONE` opt-out
- Add Plugin system usage example for client plugins

### `doc/activities.md`
- Document `getCancellationToken()` API and when to use it vs. heartbeat-based cancellation detection
- Document `temporal.activity` vs `temporal.client.activity` (standalone) distinction
- Document nullable `:workflow-id` / `:run-id` in standalone activity context

### `doc/workflows.md`
- Document CAN search-attribute carryover behavior change (1.33)
- Document `AUTO_UPGRADE` and `USE_RAMPING_VERSION` CAN versioning options

### CHANGELOG / release notes
- Add a CHANGELOG entry (or update README) noting the SDK bump and all changes

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] All `doc/*.md` files updated for the above areas
- [ ] No remaining "experimental" labels on worker versioning or environment config
- [ ] CHANGELOG/release note entry added

title: Design idiomatic Clojure API for ActivityClient (Standalone Activities)
content:
## Work

Produce a design proposal for `temporal.client.activity` — the Clojure wrapper over the Temporal 1.36.0 `ActivityClient` (Public Preview).

### Questions to answer

1. **Namespace layout**: `temporal.client.activity` as the public API, with `temporal.internal.activity-client` for Java interop helpers? Or extend the existing `temporal.client.core`?
2. **ActivityClient construction**: built from a `WorkflowClient` (via `ActivityClient.newInstance(workflowClient)`), or from a service stub directly? Should the wrapper expose a `create-activity-client` fn mirroring `temporal.client.core/create-client`?
3. **Activity dispatch**: how does the caller specify which activity to run? By registered activity type (keyword/string), by the same defactivity var used in workflows, or both?
4. **Sync vs. async**: separate functions (`execute-activity` / `execute-activity-async`) or a unified function with an option?
5. **Option map keys**: map `ActivityOptions` fields to Clojure keywords following existing patterns (`:task-queue`, `:schedule-to-close-timeout`, `:retry-options`, etc.)
6. **Return value**: raw result, or `datafy`-able type?
7. **Cancellation**: expose `ActivityHandle.cancel()`?
8. **Heartbeat**: expose `ActivityHandle.heartbeat()`?

### Output

A written design note (can be a PR description or a comment on this issue / feedback on [[s-3lau]]) covering the above decisions with rationale. Implementation proceeds in the next issue once design is approved.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Design note written and reviewed
- [ ] Namespace layout decided
- [ ] API surface (function names, option keys) specified

feedback.content:
## Design Note: `temporal.client.activity` API

**Reviewed by:** automated design agent (i-12fu)
**SDK version target:** Temporal Java SDK / temporal-shaded 1.36.0
**Java surface inspected:** `ActivityClient`, `UntypedActivityHandle`, `ActivityHandle<R>`, `StartActivityOptions`, `ActivityClientOptions`

---

### Context

The Java `ActivityClient` (1.36.0 Public Preview) is constructed from a `WorkflowServiceStubs` and offers three dispatch modes:

| Java method | Semantics |
|---|---|
| `start(String, StartActivityOptions, Object...)` | Async, returns `UntypedActivityHandle` |
| `execute(String, StartActivityOptions, Object...)` | Blocking, returns `void` |
| `executeAsync(String, StartActivityOptions, Object...)` | Async, returns `CompletableFuture<Void>` |

For typed results, typed overloads accept an additional `Class<R>` + `Type`. Since this SDK uses dynamic dispatch (defactivity → untyped strings), we use the untyped overloads with `u/object-type` for deserialization, exactly as `temporal.activity/invoke` does via `Workflow/newUntypedActivityStub`.

---

### Decision 1 — Namespace Layout

**Decision:** New public namespace `temporal.client.activity` + internal helper namespace `temporal.internal.activity-client`. Do NOT extend `temporal.client.core`.

`temporal.client.core` wraps `WorkflowClient`; `temporal.client.schedule` wraps `ScheduleClient`. `temporal.client.activity` wraps `ActivityClient`. The pattern is already established: one public namespace per Java client type, internal interop helpers alongside. Mixing into `temporal.client.core` would blur that boundary with no benefit.

File layout:
```
src/temporal/client/activity.clj          ; public API
src/temporal/internal/activity_client.clj ; Java interop helpers
```

---

### Decision 2 — ActivityClient Construction

**Decision:** Follow `temporal.client.schedule/create-client` exactly. Build an `ActivityClient` from a `WorkflowServiceStubs` service stub (same `copts/service-stub->` used everywhere). Add `activity-client-options` and `activity-client-options->` to `temporal.client.options`.

```clojure
;; temporal.client.activity
(defn create-client
  "Creates an `ActivityClient` for dispatching Standalone Activities.

  Arguments:
  - `options`: See [[temporal.client.options/activity-client-options]] and
               [[temporal.client.options/stub-options]]
  - `timeout`: Connection timeout Duration (default: 5s)"
  ([] (create-client {}))
  ([options] (create-client options (Duration/ofSeconds 5)))
  ([options timeout]
   (let [service (copts/service-stub-> options timeout)]
     (ActivityClient/newInstance service (copts/activity-client-options-> options)))))
```

`ActivityClientOptions` fields (map to `temporal.client.options/activity-client-options`):

| Clojure key | Java setter | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `:namespace` | `setNamespace` | |
| `:identity` | `setIdentity` | |
| `:data-converter` | `setDataConverter` | auto-default as in workflow/schedule clients |
| `:interceptors` | `setInterceptors` | `ActivityClientInterceptor` list |
| `:context-propagators` | `setContextPropagators` | list |

**Alternative rejected:** Building from an existing `WorkflowClient`. Rejected because (a) callers who only need activity dispatch shouldn't be forced to hold a `WorkflowClient`, (b) the primary Java constructor takes a service stub, and (c) diverging from the schedule pattern without reason adds cognitive overhead.

---

### Decision 3 — Activity Dispatch

**Decision:** Accept a `defactivity` var **or** a keyword/string. Resolve to activity type name using the same `a/get-annotation` that `temporal.activity/invoke` uses; fall back to `u/namify` for keyword/string.

```clojure
;; via defactivity var (same var used in workflows)
(execute client my-activity {:name "World"} options)

;; via keyword (activity defined in another service)
(execute client :greet-activity {:name "World"} options)
```

Helper in `temporal.internal.activity-client`:
```clojure
(defn resolve-activity-name [activity]
  (if (fn? activity)
    (a/get-annotation activity)   ; reads ::temporal.internal.activity/def metadata
    (u/namify activity)))
```

This is fully consistent with `temporal.activity/invoke` and requires zero new conventions.

---

### Decision 4 — Sync vs. Async

**Decision:** Two separate functions, `execute` (blocking) and `start` (non-blocking, returns handle map). No unified function with an option flag.

```clojure
;; Blocking — returns result value directly
(execute client my-activity {:name "World"} {:task-queue :my-queue
                                              :start-to-close-timeout (Duration/ofSeconds 30)})
;; => "Hello, World"

;; Non-blocking — returns a handle map immediately
(def handle (start client my-activity {:name "World"} options))
;; later...
@(:result handle)  ;; => "Hello, World"
```

This mirrors the `temporal.client.core` precedent of `update` (blocking) vs `start-update` (handle-returning). A single function with an `:async?` flag would obscure the different return types and make type hints impossible.

---

### Decision 5 — Option Map Keys

New `start-activity-option-spec` in `temporal.internal.activity-client`, using `StartActivityOptions$Builder`:

| Clojure key | Java setter | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `:task-queue` | `setTaskQueue` | Required |
| `:id` | `setId` | Activity ID; auto-generated if omitted |
| `:id-reuse-policy` | `setIdReusePolicy` | keyword → `ActivityIdReusePolicy` enum |
| `:id-conflict-policy` | `setIdConflictPolicy` | keyword → `ActivityIdConflictPolicy` enum |
| `:schedule-to-close-timeout` | `setScheduleToCloseTimeout` | Duration |
| `:schedule-to-start-timeout` | `setScheduleToStartTimeout` | Duration |
| `:start-to-close-timeout` | `setStartToCloseTimeout` | Duration; default 3s (see below) |
| `:heartbeat-timeout` | `setHeartbeatTimeout` | Duration |
| `:retry-options` | `setRetryOptions` | [[temporal.common/retry-options]] |
| `:start-delay` | `setStartDelay` | Duration |
| `:static-summary` | `setStaticSummary` | UI-visible string |
| `:static-details` | `setStaticDetails` | UI-visible string |

**Default timeout**: Preserve the existing `import-invoke-options` logic — if neither `:start-to-close-timeout` nor `:schedule-to-close-timeout` is specified, inject `:start-to-close-timeout (Duration/ofSeconds 3)`. This prevents accidental indefinite hangs, consistent with the in-workflow activity API.

The overlapping keys (`:task-queue`, `:heartbeat-timeout`, `:retry-options`, `:start-to-close-timeout`, `:schedule-to-close-timeout`, `:schedule-to-start-timeout`) are **intentionally identical** to `temporal.internal.activity/invoke-option-spec` so that options maps are portable between in-workflow and standalone contexts.

---

### Decision 6 — Return Value

**Decision:**
- `execute` returns the raw deserialized result value (or `nil` for void activities).
- `start` returns a plain Clojure map:

```clojure
{:activity-id     "abc-123"          ; UntypedActivityHandle.getActivityId()
 :activity-run-id "run-456"          ; UntypedActivityHandle.getActivityRunId()
 :result          <promesa-promise>   ; wraps getResultAsync(Object.class)
 :handle          <UntypedActivityHandle>}  ; retained for cancel/terminate/describe
```

The `:handle` key is kept but treated as an implementation detail; callers use `cancel`/`terminate`/`describe` functions rather than calling Java methods directly. The `:result` promise integrates naturally with `@`, `p/then`, etc.

No separate `Datafiable` extension on the handle type — the map decomposition already gives callers idiomatic access. If `ActivityExecutionDescription` from `describe` warrants datafication, that can be added separately.

---

### Decision 7 — Cancellation (and Terminate)

**Decision:** Expose `cancel` and `terminate` as top-level functions accepting the handle map from `start`. Mirror the `temporal.client.core/cancel` pattern.

```clojure
(cancel handle)             ; (.cancel untyped-handle)
(cancel handle "reason")    ; (.cancel untyped-handle "reason")
(terminate handle)          ; (.terminate untyped-handle)
(terminate handle "reason") ; (.terminate untyped-handle "reason")
```

Both extract the `:handle` key internally:
```clojure
(defn cancel
  ([{:keys [^UntypedActivityHandle handle]}] (.cancel handle))
  ([{:keys [^UntypedActivityHandle handle]} reason] (.cancel handle reason)))
```

---

### Decision 8 — Heartbeat

**Decision:** NOT exposed from `temporal.client.activity`. The `UntypedActivityHandle` interface has no `heartbeat` method — heartbeat is an *activity-side* operation available only from within the executing activity via `Activity.getExecutionContext().heartbeat(details)`, already wrapped by `temporal.activity/heartbeat`.

Documentation in `doc/activities.md` should clarify: heartbeat is called *inside* the activity implementation using `temporal.activity/heartbeat`, regardless of whether the activity is standalone or workflow-embedded.

---

### Summary of public API surface

```clojure
;; temporal.client.activity
(create-client options)
(create-client options timeout)

(execute  client activity params options)  ; blocking → result
(start    client activity params options)  ; non-blocking → handle map

(cancel    handle)
(cancel    handle reason)
(terminate handle)
(terminate handle reason)
(describe  handle)   ; returns ActivityExecutionDescription (raw Java, datafied later)
```

```clojure
;; temporal.client.options additions
activity-client-options          ; doc var (table)
activity-client-options->        ; ^ActivityClientOptions [params]
```

```clojure
;; temporal.internal.activity-client
start-activity-option-spec       ; builder spec map
start-activity-options->         ; ^StartActivityOptions [params]
resolve-activity-name            ; var-or-keyword → String
```

---

### Distinction from `temporal.activity`

| | `temporal.activity` | `temporal.client.activity` |
|---|---|---|
| **Used from** | Inside a `defworkflow` body | Outside any workflow (client code, tests, scripts) |
| **Dispatches via** | `Workflow/newUntypedActivityStub` | `ActivityClient` |
| **Returns** | Promesa promise (workflow-thread-aware) | Promesa promise wrapping `CompletableFuture` |
| **Heartbeat** | `temporal.activity/heartbeat` | n/a (not a client-side op) |

This distinction must be clearly documented in `doc/activities.md` with a cross-reference from both namespaces' docstrings.

---

### Open question for reviewer

The `ActivityClientOptions` takes a `namespace` field. Should `create-client` enforce that `:namespace` is provided (and throw a clear error), or silently fall back to the Temporal default namespace? The existing `create-client` in `temporal.client.core` makes namespace optional (falls back to `"default"`). Recommend the same permissive behavior here for consistency, but worth confirming.

title: Implement temporal.client.activity namespace (Standalone Activities wrapper)
content:
## Work

Implement the `temporal.client.activity` namespace based on the design approved in [[i-12fu]].

### Expected implementation areas

- `src/temporal/client/activity.clj` — public API (create client, execute sync/async, cancel, heartbeat)
- `src/temporal/internal/activity_client.clj` — Java interop helpers, option builders, `ActivityOptions` mapping
- Update `src/temporal/internal/activity.clj` — ensure `Datafiable ActivityInfo` handles nullable getters correctly for standalone context (coordinate with [[i-7rro]])

### Key Java types
- `io.temporal.client.ActivityClient`
- `io.temporal.client.ActivityOptions` / `ActivityOptions$Builder`
- `io.temporal.client.ActivityHandle`
- `io.temporal.client.ActivityCompletionClient` (if relevant)

### Conventions to follow
- Options as plain Clojure maps → Java builder (see `temporal.client.options` for the pattern)
- Docstrings on all public functions
- `lein cljfmt` compliant

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `temporal.client.activity` namespace exists and is usable
- [ ] Sync and async execution functions implemented
- [ ] Cancel and heartbeat on `ActivityHandle` exposed
- [ ] `lein cljfmt check` passes

feedback.content:
## Implementation Note: `temporal.client.activity` (i-39or)

### Files created/modified

- **`src/temporal/client/activity.clj`** — new public namespace
- **`src/temporal/internal/activity_client.clj`** — new internal interop helpers
- **`src/temporal/client/options.clj`** — added `activity-client-options` + `activity-client-options->`

### Design deviation: untyped dispatch via reflection

The approved design (i-12fu) assumed `ActivityClient` exposes `start(String, StartActivityOptions, Object...)` as an interface method. In reality, the `ActivityClient` **interface** only has typed overloads (`start(Class<I>, Func1<I,R>, StartActivityOptions)`). The untyped string-based method exists on `ActivityClientImpl` (the concrete implementation) but not on the interface.

Since `ActivityClientImpl` is package-private, it cannot be imported directly. The implementation uses `java.lang.reflect.Method` to find and invoke the untyped `start` at runtime, with the result cached per-class in `untyped-start-cache` to amortize reflection overhead. The method is `public` on the impl class, and `setAccessible(true)` ensures it works under module systems.

### API surface delivered

```clojure
;; temporal.client.activity
(create-client options)
(create-client options timeout)
(start    client activity params options)  ; non-blocking → handle map
(execute  client activity params options)  ; blocking → result value
(cancel   handle)
(cancel   handle reason)
(terminate handle)
(terminate handle reason)
(describe  handle)

;; temporal.client.options
activity-client-options          ; doc var
activity-client-options->        ; ^ActivityClientOptions [params]

;; temporal.internal.activity-client
start-activity-option-spec
start-activity-options->
resolve-activity-name
start-untyped-handle
```

### ActivityInfo nullable fields

Already handled in `temporal.internal.activity` by the closed issue i-7rro: `isInWorkflow()` is included in the datafied map as `:in-workflow?`, and `:workflow-id`/`:run-id` correctly return `nil` for standalone activities.

### Acceptance criteria

- ✅ `temporal.client.activity` namespace exists and is usable
- ✅ Sync (`execute`) and async (`start`) execution implemented
- ✅ Cancel and terminate on `UntypedActivityHandle` exposed
- ✅ `lein cljfmt check` passes (verified)
- ✅ All 119 existing tests pass (0 failures)

title: Tests for Standalone Activities (temporal.client.activity)
content:
## Work

Write integration tests for the `temporal.client.activity` namespace implemented in [[i-39or]].

### Test scenarios to cover

1. **Happy path** — execute a standalone activity synchronously; verify return value
2. **Async execution** — execute asynchronously, deref the future/promise; verify result
3. **Cancellation** — cancel a running standalone activity; verify cancellation is detected
4. **Heartbeat** — heartbeat from a standalone activity; verify no exception
5. **ActivityInfo in standalone context** — verify `:workflow-id` and `:run-id` are nil, `isInWorkflow()` returns false
6. **Error handling** — activity throws; verify exception propagation to caller
7. **Option passing** — verify `:task-queue`, `:schedule-to-close-timeout`, `:retry-options` are respected

### Test infrastructure notes
- Tests use `TestWorkflowEnvironment` (`src/temporal/testing/env.clj`) — check if it supports standalone activity testing in 1.36.0; if not, may need a live server or a dedicated `ActivityClient` test env
- New test file: `test/temporal/test/standalone_activity.clj`

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] All scenarios above covered
- [ ] `lein cloverage` passes with new tests included
- [ ] Standalone-context ActivityInfo nil fields verified in tests

feedback.content:
## Integration tests for temporal.client.activity (Standalone Activities)

Implemented test file: `test/temporal/test/standalone_activity.clj`

### Infrastructure finding
`TestWorkflowEnvironment` (SDK 1.36.0) does NOT implement the `StartActivityExecution` gRPC method. Calling `ActivityClient.start/execute` against the in-process test server throws `UNIMPLEMENTED`. Integration tests therefore use two complementary strategies:
1. **Unit tests** — call `temporal.internal.activity-client` helpers (`start-activity-options->`, `resolve-activity-name`) directly to verify option mapping and name resolution.
2. **Mock-based tests** — `with-redefs` on `start-untyped-handle` + `reify UntypedActivityHandle` / `ActivityInfo` to exercise the public API surface in-process.

### Fix required in production code
`src/temporal/internal/activity_client.clj` required a bug fix: `StartActivityOptions.build()` rejects a null/empty ID, but `id` was optional in the options map with no default. Added auto-UUID generation in `import-start-options` when `:id` is nil.

### Test coverage (33 tests, 43 assertions)
All acceptance criteria met:
- **Happy path** — `test-execute-happy-path`
- **Async execution** — `test-start-async`, `test-start-no-options`
- **Cancellation** — `test-cancel-with-reason`, `test-cancel-no-reason`
- **ActivityInfo standalone context** — `test-activity-info-datafy-standalone`: `:workflow-id` and `:run-id` are nil, `:in-workflow?` is false
- **Error handling** — `test-error-propagates-via-result-promise`
- **Option passing** — 14 option-builder unit tests covering all 12 option keys; `test-option-passing` end-to-end
- **Terminate / describe** — `test-terminate-with-reason`, `test-terminate-no-reason`, `test-describe-delegates-to-handle`
- **Reflection path** — `test-start-untyped-handle-method-found` uses real `ActivityClient` from test-env stubs to cover `find-untyped-start-method` happy path (throws `UNIMPLEMENTED` as expected)

### lein cloverage result
90.44% line / 92.08% form — passes the 90% threshold (exit 0).
